title: Add-Type C# code references System.Drawing types without conditional assembly loading
description: In .NET 6+ (PowerShell 7), System.Drawing.Color lives in System.Drawing.Primitives, not System.Drawing. Any Add-Type C# code using System.Drawing.Color must conditionally include the correct assembly based on PS version. Add-Type compilation failures are terminating errors that bypass -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -- must use try/catch. Previously caused PwShGUI-Theme.psm1 import failure.
detection_method: Regex scan for Add-Type calls using System.Drawing types without version-conditional assembly references
preventionRule: All Add-Type calls referencing System.Drawing must dynamically build the -ReferencedAssemblies list based on $PSVersionTable.PSVersion.Major. PS 5.x uses System.Drawing; PS 7+ uses System.Drawing.Primitives.
remedy: Wrap Add-Type in a version check: if ($PSVersionTable.PSVersion.Major -ge 7) { add System.Drawing.Primitives } else { add System.Drawing }. Use try/catch, not -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue.
